dramatic_function: Establish maritime city, labour and civic scale
Announce festival and its promise of order and abundance
Contrast public celebration with private renunciation and inherited grief
Reveal wealth, religious plurality and those excluded from prosperity
Reveal Manimekalai's beauty, social visibility and vulnerability to pursuit
Show Manimekalai's transformation through practical service and the prince's inability to accept it
Make Manimekalai's disguise legible to the audience and credible to both Udayakumaran and Kanchanan
Make famine visible through repair, sale, reuse and unequal preservation of clothing and ornament
Establish intellectual plurality and civic rivalry before Manimekalai's formal philosophical journey
Give Manimekalai access to rival schools while revealing that authority is socially read through body and performance
Stage the strongest challenge to rebirth and force Manimekalai to distinguish memory from proof
Teach inference and fallacy through an urgent observable problem rather than a catalogue of logical terms
Connect dependent causation to crop failure, price, storage, illness, grief and ethical intervention
Complete Manimekalai's movement from hearing doctrines to disciplined practice without declaring that history itself has ended
